title: The One Where Rachel Smokes

jokes.text: Is that your card? / Yes. [immediately after being asked to memorize it]
jokes.setup: Joey performs a magic trick asking someone to memorize a card
jokes.punchline: He immediately reveals the 'wrong' card but gets confirmation it's right, suggesting either terrible magic or lucky guess
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey incompetence played for laughs. Well-structured with economy of setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Ben takes one lousy walk in the park and gets an audition? I mean, way to go, Ben! I've been in that park a million times and no one's offered me an audition.
jokes.setup: Joey learns Ben got discovered for a commercial in the park
jokes.punchline: Joey's jealousy of a toddler's career success, delivered with genuine frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his actor insecurity applied to competing with a baby is both relatable and absurd.

jokes.text: I'm always on the swings! What am I doing wrong? / That.
jokes.setup: Joey asks what he's doing wrong to not get discovered
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate deadpan 'That' implies Joey's current behavior is exactly the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word punchline that lands hard. Classic Chandler timing and Joey obliviousness.

jokes.text: I don't know if I'd feel comfortable stealing on my very first day.
jokes.setup: Asked if she can get free Ralph Lauren shirts from work
jokes.punchline: Rachel's moral concern is specifically about timing (first day) not the stealing itself
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing Rachel's priorities, though not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Unwilling to steal from work. Interesting. If anybody gets free stuff, it's me.
jokes.setup: Rachel won't steal from Ralph Lauren
jokes.punchline: Phoebe notes this as unusual and claims priority on any stolen goods
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe logic and moral flexibility. Decent character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: If we throw her party on her birthday, it's not a surprise.
jokes.setup: Planning Rachel's surprise birthday party a month early
jokes.punchline: Monica's logic that the surprise element requires wrong timing
jokes.explanation: Monica's over-planning personality leads to genuinely twisted logic that somehow makes sense.

jokes.text: They're gonna do it together. / That's my girlfriend. / So I gotta shut it down now?
jokes.setup: Monica and Phoebe excitedly agree to plan the party together
jokes.punchline: Chandler's jealousy over Monica's enthusiasm for collaborating with someone else
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Chandler's insecurity about Monica's friendships.

jokes.text: Weirder than seeing his two moms make out?
jokes.setup: Ross says it'll be weird seeing an actor play Ben's dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler references the even weirder reality of Ben's lesbian mothers
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback that puts Ross's concern in perspective with show history.

jokes.text: You're not his godfather. / What? Are you kidding? Of course I am.
jokes.setup: Joey confidently claims to be Ben's godfather
jokes.punchline: Ross's flat denial and Joey's shocked disbelief
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's assumptions vs. reality, though fairly standard sitcom beat.

jokes.text: So I steered clear of her. And the other one, I know from... Well, you know.
jokes.setup: Joey explains his strategy with the casting directors
jokes.punchline: Implies he slept with one casting director, delivered as matter-of-fact
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey sexual history joke, well-integrated with his character but predictable.

jokes.text: He was so good, he convinced me to switch phone companies. Chandler was mad.
jokes.setup: Talking about a kid actor they recognize from commercials
jokes.punchline: Ross was so influenced by a child's performance he changed phone service, annoying Chandler
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on advertising effectiveness with personal consequence. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: I really don't know you well enough for you to do that.
jokes.setup: Joey and Ross are getting competitive about who looks more like Ben
jokes.punchline: Ross's uncomfortable response to Joey's presumed intimate gesture or comment
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment, though we can only infer what Joey did from Ross's reaction.

jokes.text: Then I take credit for them. I'm kidding! / She is kidding. But don't ever disagree with her again. / Okay, now I'm kidding!
jokes.setup: Rachel's boss explains her job includes giving opinions
jokes.punchline: Escalating jokes about credit-stealing and consequences that become increasingly unclear what's serious
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalating workplace power dynamic joke with good confusion payoff.

jokes.text: About ghosts and goblins... who totally supported the princess' right to smoke.
jokes.setup: Rachel starts to explain her doctor father's horror stories about smoking
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'horror stories' were actually fairy tales with pro-smoking messages
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that completely subverts expectation. Creative absurdist take on anti-smoking messaging.

jokes.text: who do you think she'll pick? Me or Smokey Smokerson?
jokes.setup: Rachel worries about promotion competition with smoking colleagues
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous name 'Smokey Smokerson' for her cigarette-enabled rival
jokes.explanation: Fun made-up name that captures Rachel's dramatic interpretation of workplace dynamics.

jokes.text: One of the old-timers told me there used to be a little tree in there.
jokes.setup: Describing the old workplace smoking area with dirt for cigarette disposal
jokes.punchline: Implies the accumulated cigarette butts killed a plant over time
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny environmental consequence joke delivered with Phoebe's matter-of-fact style.

jokes.text: Wow, I still don't have my lung capacity back yet.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is encouraging Rachel to smoke
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions her own ongoing health problems from smoking while promoting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe contradiction - giving harmful advice while acknowledging the harm to herself.

jokes.text: Oh, I changed my mind.
jokes.setup: Ross eagerly agrees to join Monica and Phoebe for coffee
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs out, presumably after seeing their intense party planning energy
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ross's self-preservation instincts around Monica's planning mode.

jokes.text: I've highlighted the ones that'll go with the food.
jokes.setup: Monica presents elaborate party planning materials
jokes.punchline: Has color-coordinated music selection with menu items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica over-organization taken to absurd logical extreme. Peak character integration.

jokes.text: Cups and ice? I get to be in charge of cups and ice? / All right. Fine. I will be in charge of cups and ice. / I can get ice at the restaurant / I got it!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is relegated to menial party tasks
jokes.punchline: Her increasing indignation and territorial claiming of the minimal responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good escalating frustration comedy that sets up later payoff with elaborate cup displays.

jokes.text: Are you doing Joey's: 'The audition didn't go so well... Yeah, it did'? / Yeah, I am!
jokes.setup: Ross appears to be giving bad news about the audition
jokes.punchline: Chandler recognizes Ross is copying Joey's signature fake-out delivery style
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about character patterns being recognizable and copyable within the show's universe.

jokes.text: You know what I mean? Doobies?
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to cover for not knowing her coworkers smoke regular cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Uses outdated slang 'doobies' showing how out of touch she is with drug culture
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Rachel's privileged background through awkward slang usage.

jokes.text: We get high. / Me too. / I'm kidding. / Me too.
jokes.setup: Workplace conversation about getting high
jokes.punchline: Escalating admissions and denials creating confusion about who's serious
jokes.explanation: Good escalating workplace awkwardness with Rachel trying to fit in unsuccessfully.

jokes.text: I'm not married to him anymore. He's my ex-husband. I'm totally gay!
jokes.setup: Susan appears panicked about something at the audition
jokes.punchline: Over-clarifies her relationship status and sexuality to casting directors
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to character history with workplace inappropriateness cringe factor.

jokes.text: No. We're like best friends. That's why it's weird. / I thought we were just talking.
jokes.setup: Discussion about competing against each other being weird
jokes.punchline: Joey and Ross have completely different understandings of their relationship level
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy showing different emotional investment levels between friends.

jokes.text: Yeah, okay, but I have been supporting a 29-year-old Italian for five years.
jokes.setup: Asked about wanting free soup when a can costs 60 cents
jokes.punchline: References supporting Joey financially as justification for wanting free soup
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes their relationship dynamic with perfect economy and character truth.

jokes.text: Good, because we got Rachel 800 gallons of water.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions bringing lots of cups
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan response about proportionally absurd amount of water
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist math joke delivered with Chandler's signature deadpan style.

jokes.text: You know, I rued the day once. Didn't get a whole lot else done.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Monica will 'rue the day' about cup assignment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal interpretation of 'ruing' as a time-consuming activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that treats an idiom literally with perfect Chandler delivery. Highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: I just said that so you wouldn't let Ben do it. If anyone should step aside, it should be Ben.
jokes.setup: Joey appears to make a noble sacrifice by stepping aside
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it was manipulation and now wants the baby to step aside
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey selfishness reveal with good structure. Shows his competitive nature overriding friendship.

jokes.text: Hi, no one!
jokes.setup: Chandler goes to answer the door during argument
jokes.punchline: Delivers cheerful greeting to empty doorway
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Chandler's sarcastic politeness to nobody.

jokes.text: You're like the tagalong dad!
jokes.setup: Joey and Ross arguing about the audition
jokes.punchline: Joey creates specific insult about Ross's secondary status as Ben's father figure
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that captures the awkward family dynamic with Joey's characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: Come on! Have you seen what my kid can do? He dials phones, he eats tortilla chips. He plays soccer with the cartoon tiger!
jokes.setup: Joey defending his hypothetical kid against Ben
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly specific and absurd child actor achievements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list of hyper-specific commercial actions. Great absurdist comedy with perfect specificity.

jokes.text: You just give him a spoon, baby.
jokes.setup: Asked if his kid eats soup better than Ben
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident, condescending dismissal of Ben's soup-eating technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey cockiness about something absurd, delivered with dismissive expertise about toddler soup consumption.

jokes.text: This commercial belongs to me and Mitch! / Your kid's name is Raymond! / Yeah? So is yours!
jokes.setup: Joey and Ross getting increasingly heated about the commercial
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely wrong name claim followed by doubling down with same wrong name
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating stupidity with Joey's confident wrongness. Perfect character comedy showing his heated irrationality.

jokes.text: You stink! / Thanks. / No, I'm serious. That's because I went down there and they were smoking. This is actually the smell of success.
jokes.setup: Monica comments on Rachel's smell
jokes.punchline: Rachel misinterprets as compliment then reframes cigarette smell as professional achievement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Rachel's optimistic reframing of obvious problem as career advancement.

jokes.text: You look happy and sick. You smoked!
jokes.setup: Monica observing Rachel's condition
jokes.punchline: Identifies the contradictory emotional and physical state of first-time smoking
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy about the specific contradiction of workplace smoking satisfaction vs. physical discomfort.

jokes.text: Yeah, my tongue feels a little fuzzy and my fingers sort of smell. I actually feel like I could throw up.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending smoking as 'not that bad'
jokes.punchline: Immediately lists multiple unpleasant physical symptoms while maintaining it's fine
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction comedy showing Rachel's denial through detailed symptom listing.

jokes.text: You smell so smoky, I gotta get up. / I'm not kidding. / I think you smell great.
jokes.setup: Monica complains about Rachel's cigarette smell
jokes.punchline: Chandler's contradictory romantic appreciation of the same smell Monica finds repulsive
jokes.explanation: Decent relationship humor showing Chandler's contrarian attraction to things others find off-putting.

jokes.text: Great! Give me those cigs. Come on! Give it! Out. Done. Quit. / My late husband gave me that lighter. / I'm not kidding. / Okay, then.
jokes.setup: Enthusiastic group decision to quit smoking with Rachel taking charge
jokes.punchline: Immediate emotional manipulation about sentimental lighter value stopping Rachel cold
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of enthusiasm through unexpected emotional stakes. Solid structure and escalation.

jokes.text: So you want to be an actor? I gotta tell you, it's no picnic. There's tons of rejection. No stability... one day, you're Dr. Drake Ramoray. The next day, you're eating ketchup out of the bottle.
jokes.setup: Joey giving career advice to another child at the audition
jokes.punchline: References his own soap opera role and current financial struggles with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration with multiple show callbacks. Perfect Joey giving terrible advice with personal examples.

jokes.text: Oh, who am I kidding. I can't talk you out of this. It's a great life.
jokes.setup: Joey listing all the negatives of acting career
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal as he realizes he's actually selling the lifestyle he enjoys
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Joey's inability to genuinely discourage someone from his chosen path.

jokes.text: What'd I say? / Mmm...noodle soup. / How's that different?
jokes.setup: Joey is told the line is 'Mmm...soup' but keeps saying 'noodle soup'
jokes.punchline: Joey's genuine confusion about the difference between the two phrases
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness to obvious distinction. Shows his acting limitations through simple line reading failure.

jokes.text: Mmm...noodle soup. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Joey repeatedly trying and failing to say the correct line
jokes.punchline: Even when trying to curse his mistake, he still says the wrong line
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Joey's incompetence. Perfect comedy of actor unable to perform simplest task.

jokes.text: I mean, I have patches all over my body, you know? I mean, I haven't slept in two days. / We quit yesterday.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing intense withdrawal symptoms
jokes.punchline: Kim's matter-of-fact reminder that they only quit yesterday
jokes.explanation: Good deflation comedy showing Rachel's dramatic overreaction to minimal time period.

jokes.text: That's counting today.
jokes.setup: Reminded they only quit yesterday
jokes.punchline: Rachel clarifies she's including today in her day count, making it even more pathetic
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up escalation showing Rachel's desperate attempt to make her suffering seem more significant.

jokes.text: Busted! Come on! What are you doing? I thought we were the Patch Sisters.
jokes.setup: Rachel catches her coworkers smoking after they agreed to quit
jokes.punchline: References their earlier 'Patch Sisters' solidarity pact
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier group identity, showing Rachel's investment in their failed pact.

jokes.text: Cup hat, cup banner, cup chandelier, and the thing that started it all: the cup!
jokes.setup: Phoebe was put in charge of cups and ice for the party
jokes.punchline: Created elaborate cup-themed decorations in escalating absurdity ending with basic cup
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and visual payoff for earlier setup. Perfect Phoebe over-commitment to absurd task.

jokes.text: Mystical.
jokes.setup: Phoebe demonstrates dry ice effects
jokes.punchline: Chandler's flat, unimpressed response to the supposedly impressive display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan economy. One word that deflates Phoebe's enthusiasm effectively.

jokes.text: No one's eating my food. They're eating her snow cones!
jokes.setup: Monica's elaborate party planning being overshadowed
jokes.punchline: Her gourmet menu defeated by Phoebe's simple frozen treats
jokes.explanation: Good Monica competitiveness and frustration at being upstaged by simpler approach.

jokes.text: What? My birthday is not for another month. / That's the surprise.
jokes.setup: Rachel points out it's not her birthday
jokes.punchline: Monica's callback to her earlier logic about surprise timing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off Monica's earlier absurd reasoning about surprise parties.

jokes.text: I mean, Chandler's birthday is even before mine. / Surprise!
jokes.setup: Rachel noting Chandler's birthday comes first
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately throws him a surprise party too, following Monica's logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the surprise party absurdity, showing the logical extreme of Monica's reasoning.

jokes.text: What's in your hand? / Money. I'm stealing all the money.
jokes.setup: Rachel catching Chandler with something in his hand
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact admission to obviously false grand theft
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler deflection through absurd over-confession. Perfect character response to being caught.

jokes.text: But just one, okay? Because nobody knows when my birthday is.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking for just one cigarette
jokes.punchline: His sad justification based on birthday anonymity
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating Chandler moment about his social insignificance, perfect character touch.

jokes.text: They were menthol.
jokes.setup: Chandler being confronted about smoking cigarettes
jokes.punchline: His weak justification that the cigarette type makes it less bad
jokes.explanation: Decent Chandler rationalization joke, though fairly standard sitcom excuse-making.

jokes.text: What kind of an actor can't even say, 'Mmm...noodle soup'?
jokes.setup: Joey feeling bad about his failed audition
jokes.punchline: Still says the wrong line even when criticizing himself for saying the wrong line
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Joey STILL can't get the line right even in self-reflection. Perfect character consistency and structure.

jokes.text: It's not that I'm a bad actor. No, it's just that I care so damn much about little Ben that it was more important for me to see him succeed.
jokes.setup: Ross suggests Joey subconsciously sabotaged himself for Ben's sake
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately adopts this face-saving explanation with full conviction
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Joey's eagerness to accept any explanation that preserves his ego.

tmdb_overview: Rachel takes up smoking to attend important meetings; Monica and Phoebe plan Rachel's birthday party.
